ab: marelrang mi iniyam i inani mukuwa muzangiya harem kana wari.
en: I went out with my mother today.

ab: tremangiyangiya na lingatr a ’uninan, ’azi vi’as zi ’azi lritek na wari, say na vali mu, valiyan.
ch: 白天的天空很藍，天氣不冷也不熱，風很涼爽。
en: The sky is blue during the day, the weather is neither cold nor hot, and the wind is cool.

ab: kemayan mi kana tralutralun mekan za pang, tu ’uninay mi kana ’unin, salaw sadeku.
ch: 我們在草地上吃著麵包，陽光灑在身上，非常溫暖。
en: We ate bread on the grass, the sun was shining on us, and it was very warm.

ab: a piya kare’alri, demuni’ na lingatr.
en: In the afternoon, the sky turned red.

ab: kiumalr ku kani inani, kemuda zi demuni’ na lingatr?
ch: 我問媽媽，為甚麼天空是紅色的。
en: I asked my mother why the sky is red.

ab: kema i inani, kamawan za zazuwa a variw.
ch: 媽媽說，可能是颱風要來了。
en: Mom said, maybe the typhoon is coming.

ab: uniyan za kutrekutrem na lingatr, mupatraran lra na vitu’en.
ch: 晚上天上沒有雲，星星出來了。
en: There were no clouds in the sky at night, and the stars came out.

ab: kiyami ku kana vitu’en i dalan a muwaruma’ mi.
ch: 在回家的路上，我跟星星許願，
en: On the way home, I made a wish with the stars,

ab: kiyalrimayan za ’azi zuwa na variw, maranger ku ziya mukuwa muzangiya.
ch: 希望颱風不要來，我還想再出去玩。
en: I hope the typhoon doesn't come, I still want to go out to play again.
